title: Battery with a channel therethrough
abstract: A battery comprises a housing, which includes a first aperture and a second aperture on opposite sides of the housing and on the same axis, creating a channel therethrough. The first aperture and the second aperture and channel may receive a battery hold-down rod (e.g., a J-hook rod) therethrough to secure the battery to a golf cart, vehicle, or other object.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A battery, comprising: a housing; a first aperture on a top of the housing; a second aperture on a bottom of the housing; wherein the first and second apertures are aligned to form a channel through the housing. 2. The battery of claim 1, further comprising a hold-down rod sized so as to be receivable through the channel. 3. The battery of claim 2, wherein the hold-down rod comprises a J-hook portion. 4. The battery of claim 3, wherein the hold-down rod comprises a threaded top portion. 5. The battery of claim 1, further comprising a cap positionable over the first aperture. 6. The battery of claim 5, wherein the cap receives a threaded top portion of a hold down rod. 7. The battery of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of handles. 8. A battery, comprising: a housing; a first aperture on a top of the housing; a second aperture on a bottom of the housing; wherein the first and second apertures are aligned to form a channel through the housing; a hold-down rod positionable through the first aperture, the channel, and the second aperture; 

 the hold-down rod comprising a J-hook portion on a bottom end and a threaded top end, the J-hook configured to engage a crossbar; a cap receiving the threaded top end of the hold down rod; and a nut for securing the hold-down rod to the cap. 9. The battery of claim 9, further comprising at least one handle. 10. The battery of claim 9, comprising a plurality of handles. 11. The battery of claim 10, wherein each handle is received in a recessed portion of the housing, each handle hingedly coupled to the respective recessed portion.
